reasoning: Donald Trump was elected and has already officially enacted broad, massive tariffs as of early April 2025. According to multiple credible reports (Axios, CNBC, CRFB), tariffs of 10% were applied almost universally for a 90-day period, alongside rates as high as 125-145% on Chinese goods. These actions went into effect around April 2, which corresponds directly with the beginning of Q2 2025. Independent bodies such as the Yale Budget Lab and the Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget (CRFB) estimate that these new policies will push the U.S. trade-weighted average tariff rate well past 20%, far exceeding the 6% threshold required for a YES resolution. Given the magnitude of these tariffs and the mathematical impossibility of the weighted average staying below 6% during a fully tariffed Q2, the condition for this market resolving as YES is functionally guaranteed, barring extremely unlikely reversals or data anomalies.
